name: UnitedHealth Group
headline: UnitedHealth Group BASELINE-MISMATCH CORRECTION + ROTATION CONFIRMATION at 11.4 / Critical. Scanner showed RS=16.9 vs INDEX=11.4 (new mismatch not in scanner's flagged list — 5.5pt gap, RS_HIGH). INDEX 11.4 applied per assessor protocol. DOJ Medicare Advantage probe carries forward; Buffett heir Todd Combs departed per May 19 reporting (modest accountability signal — institutional-investor disengagement); first-ever revenue contraction Q1 2026; third-party review launched; stock -55% from ATH. No score change.

baselineCorrection.note: New baseline mismatch not flagged in scanner's mismatch list. RS=16.9, INDEX=11.4, diff=5.5 (RS_HIGH). INDEX is canonical truth per protocol. May 14-15 working baseline (11.4) confirmed against canonical.

ACC.rationale: Sustained. Third-party review launched but no external findings published. Buffett heir departure is modest signal but not dimensionally-loaded.

SYS.rationale: Sustained. First-ever revenue contraction is context for systemic decline but does not represent UHG's own SYS upgrade.

evidence.finding: Buffett heir Todd Combs departed UnitedHealth position per May 19 reporting. Institutional-investor signal of governance disengagement.
DOJ criminal + civil investigation into Medicare Advantage billing practices ongoing. Third-party review launched.
First-ever revenue contraction Q1 2026 (~2% YoY); stock -55% from ATH.

keyEvidence: Baseline correction: INDEX=11.4 applied (RS=16.9 discarded per protocol).
Buffett heir Todd Combs departure (May 19 reporting) — institutional-investor governance signal.
rationale: Baseline-mismatch correction + rotation confirmation at 11.4. The scanner's RS value of 16.9 is 5.5pt above index canonical 11.4 — this is a new mismatch not flagged in the scanner's mismatch list. Applied INDEX value per protocol. Buffett heir departure is a modest signal but not dimensionally-loaded; the third-party review remains the only proactive accountability mechanism. No material new evidence justifies dimensional change. Math: (1.75+1.25+1.5+1.25+1.5+1.25+1.5+1.625)/8 = 11.625/8 = 1.453 → ((1.453-1)/4)*100 = 11.33 → 11.4 (sub-0.5pt drift within tolerance).
whyHeadline: UnitedHealth baseline corrected (RS 16.9 to INDEX 11.4); Buffett heir Combs exits.

dominantDimension.note: ACC carries the dominant governance signal (Buffett heir departure + DOJ probe + third-party review), but evidence is sub-threshold.

nextForwardSignal.label: Third-party review findings disclosure; DOJ probe progression milestones

watches: DOJ probe progression (Medicare billing, Optum Rx)
newConductCategoryNote: Methodology observation: Scanner-vs-Index baseline mismatch resolution protocol confirms INDEX as canonical truth for assessor cycles. This UHG case (RS=16.9 vs INDEX=11.4, 5.5pt gap) was not flagged in the scanner's mismatch list, suggesting the scanner's RS value may reflect an internal-state cache that drifted post-application. Recommend hygiene pass on rotation-state.json next cycle.
boundaryWatchResolution: Not boundary-sensitive. 11.4 sits in central Critical band.

math_check.validator_check: Math drift -0.1pt (within tolerance). Baseline correction confirmed.

disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
